title: Monthly Financial Review
summary: A 60-minute monthly review to understand your numbers, catch problems early, and make informed decisions. Stop guessing and start knowing.
whenToUseIt: The first week of every month. Non-negotiable. If you skip this, you are flying blind.

steps.title: Check your cash position
steps.description: Log in to your bank accounts. Write down: total cash across all accounts, accounts receivable (money owed to you), accounts payable (money you owe). Calculate: net cash position = cash + receivables - payables.
steps.tips: This is the single most important number. If cash is declining month over month, everything else is secondary.

steps.title: Review your P&L
steps.description: Pull your profit and loss statement for the month. Check: total revenue (is it growing?), cost of goods sold (are margins stable?), operating expenses (any surprises?), net profit or loss. Compare to last month and to your budget.
steps.tips: Focus on trends, not single months. A one-month dip is noise. Three months of decline is a pattern.

steps.title: Calculate burn rate and runway
steps.description: Monthly burn rate = total expenses - total revenue (if negative, you are burning cash). Runway = total cash / monthly burn rate. If runway is less than 6 months, this is urgent.
steps.tips: Always calculate runway with zero revenue growth. Hope is not a financial strategy.
steps.aiPrompt: My monthly expenses are $[X], monthly revenue is $[Y], and I have $[Z] in the bank. Calculate my burn rate, runway in months, and tell me what actions I should consider at this runway level.

steps.title: Review unit economics
steps.description: Check your key unit metrics: Customer Acquisition Cost (total sales+marketing spend / new customers), Lifetime Value (average revenue per customer × average lifespan), LTV:CAC ratio. Healthy = 3:1 or better.

steps.title: Check collections and receivables
steps.description: Review outstanding invoices. Flag anything overdue by 30+ days. Send reminders for 15+ day invoices. Calculate your average days to collect.

steps.title: Decide 1-2 financial actions
steps.description: Based on your review, choose 1-2 actions for the next month. Examples: cut a specific expense, chase overdue invoices, raise prices, renegotiate a vendor contract. Write them down with a deadline.

expectedOutcome: A clear understanding of your financial position, burn rate, runway, and unit economics — plus 1-2 specific actions to improve your financial health.
commonMistakes: Skipping the review because you are busy — this is how founders get surprised by running out of cash
Looking only at revenue and ignoring expenses — revenue is vanity, profit is sanity, cash is reality
Not tracking unit economics — you can grow fast and still lose money on every customer
Making the review too complicated — 60 minutes with 6 steps is enough
